info.title: Canon Keeper API
info.summary: Frontend/backend contract for the Canon Keeper writer workspace.
info.description: Canonical API contract for the frontend-first Canon Keeper implementation. The contract models the frozen design surfaces: auth, chat workspace, reader, manuscript library, indexing, reader annotations, draft editing, and agent suggestions. The current frontend may use mock data behind this shape until the backend is implemented.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Registration, login, logout, and current user endpoints. Password reset and Google sign-in shown in the UI are frontend placeholders until the backend auth provider is selected.

tags.name: Books
tags.description: Books inside a saga, including import, export, and indexing state.

tags.name: Annotations
tags.description: Reader annotations shown in the notes sidebar: highlights, notes, and bookmarks.

tags.name: Chat
tags.description: Agent chat sessions, ordinary LLM streaming, trigger markers, and workspace reader links.

tags.name: Artifacts
tags.description: Structured reader reference artifacts referenced from normal assistant text.

tags.name: Reader
tags.description: Reader navigation, search, and locators used by chat artifacts and reader annotations.

tags.name: Agent
tags.description: Draft assistant suggestions and diff/accept/reject UX.

get.summary: List projects available to the user.
List books in a project shelf.

post.summary: Create a book inside a project.

get.summary: Get a book with current indexing status.

post.summary: Upload FB2/EPUB and start parsing plus indexing.

get.summary: List active or recent indexing jobs for a project.

post.summary: Cancel an import/indexing job when the UI exposes a cancel action.

get.summary: Poll a book export job and retrieve its download URL when ready.
List chapter structure for a book.
Get chapter content for reading or draft editing.

parameters.name: viewMode
parameters.description: Select reading or draft payload without changing the chapter lifecycle state.

patch.summary: Save chapter title, draft body, or ordering metadata.

post.summary: Publish the current draft revision into the reader version.

get.summary: List reader annotations anchored to a chapter.

post.summary: Create a highlight, note, or bookmark anchored to a reader selection.

get.summary: Search indexed canon, chapters, reader annotations, and materials.
List chat sessions for the project sidebar.
List messages in a chat session.

post.summary: Send a user message and receive an assistant turn, optionally as an OpenAI-style stream.
post.description: When `stream` is true, the response is a normal LLM text/event-stream with text deltas, reasoning/tool-call events, completion, and errors. Domain objects are not streamed as special events. If the assistant creates structured material, it emits a trigger marker in ordinary text, and the client fetches the relevant resource separately.

200.description: OpenAI-style streaming response. The assistant may include trigger markers in ordinary text, for example `::ck-trigger{kind="reader_references" artifactId="..."}` or `::ck-trigger{kind="agent_suggestions_ready" chapterId="..."}`.

schema.description: Server-sent events whose `data:` payloads are JSON objects following the provider-neutral `LlmStreamEvent` vocabulary: text_delta, reasoning_delta, tool_call, tool_result, completed, and error. The completed event includes `assistantMessageId` so the client can persist the final turn without guessing; clients may still refetch `/chats/{chatId}/messages` after completion.

get.summary: Get reader references referenced from assistant text.
get.description: The chat stream itself remains ordinary LLM streaming. The client detects a reader reference trigger in message text and calls this endpoint to fetch formatted reader references. Agent suggestions are separate backend entities and are fetched through the Agent endpoints.

get.summary: List draft agent suggestions for a chapter.

parameters.name: batchId
parameters.description: Use with `agent_suggestions_ready` trigger to fetch only suggestions created by that assistant turn.

post.summary: Ask the draft agent for a targeted suggestion on a chapter.

sessionAuth.name: ck_session
sessionAuth.description: Provider-neutral same-origin session cookie. The contract intentionally does not expose JWT, refresh-token, or OAuth provider mechanics.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated user does not have access to the requested project, book, chapter, annotation, chat, job, or suggestion.

Conflict.description: Resource version conflict or invalid state transition.

UnsupportedMediaType.description: Uploaded file type is not supported.

Id.description: Opaque stable identifier. Implementations may use prefixed IDs such as `book_...`, `chapter_...`, or `paragraph_...`; clients must not parse semantics from the ID string.

rememberMe.description: Visible login checkbox. Backend may use it to choose session duration without exposing token mechanics to the frontend.

activeJobId.description: Active import/indexing job used by book cards to show badges such as `9/14`.

order.description: Zero-based volume order inside the saga/project.

displayNumber.description: Visible volume number such as `I`, `II`, `III`, or `IV`.

displayLabel.description: Visible label such as `Книга II` used by cards, breadcrumbs, and references.

afterBookId.description: Insert/reorder the new volume after this book; null inserts at the beginning.
Move this volume after another book; null moves it to the beginning.

maxFileSizeBytes.description: 50 MB limit shown in the import dropzone.

file.description: FB2 or EPUB file. Must satisfy `ImportConstraints`: .fb2/.epub and max 50 MB.

currentUnit.description: Current progress unit, e.g. 9 in `9 из 14`.

totalUnits.description: Total progress units, e.g. 14 in `9 из 14`.

unitLabel.description: Human-readable unit label such as chapters, fragments, or paragraphs.

displayLabel.description: Renderable previous/next label, e.g. `11. Колокол южного ряда`.

displayNumber.description: Visible chapter number/label used in reader header/footer.

position.description: One-based chapter position in the current book.

total.description: Total chapters in the current book.

previous.description: Renderable previous chapter control; null at the beginning of a book.

next.description: Renderable next chapter control; null at the end of a book.

revision.description: Revision of the payload returned for the selected viewMode.

text.description: Plain text derived from markdown for search, references, and compact previews.

markdown.description: Canonical editable paragraph content. Supports the draft toolbar subset: bold, italic, heading, quote, list item, and link markup.

ChapterParagraphInput.description: Input shape for draft saves. Existing paragraphs keep `id`; newly inserted paragraphs omit `id` and may send `clientKey` so the frontend can reconcile the saved response.

clientKey.description: Temporary frontend key for unsaved paragraphs. Backend returns stable paragraph IDs in `ChapterParagraph` after save/indexing.

text.description: Optional plain-text mirror; backend may derive it from markdown.

markdown.description: Canonical editable paragraph content using the supported draft toolbar markdown subset.

ChapterSummary.description: Lightweight chapter structure item for TOC/sidebar/list screens. Full paragraph content is returned only by `/chapters/{chapterId}`.

displayNumber.description: Visible chapter number/label used in structure navigation.

expectedDraftRevision.description: Draft revision the frontend is publishing. Backend returns 409 if the draft changed.

TextRange.description: Optional precision range inside the paragraph identified by a surrounding ReaderLocator.

targetView.description: Text version this locator points to. Chat reader references normally use `reading`; agent suggestions and draft selections normally use `draft`.

revision.description: Optional chapter revision for stale/conflict-sensitive anchors.

paragraphId.description: Stable paragraph anchor used by assistant trigger markers when the reference points to a specific paragraph.
Required stable paragraph anchor for interactive reader references from chat/search artifacts.

quote.description: Selected text shown on highlight/note cards; null for pure bookmarks.

body.description: User note text; null for highlights/bookmarks without note text.
Optional note text attached to the highlight.

quote.description: Selected text quoted by the note when created from a reader selection.

tasks.description: Agent task chips selected in the UI, e.g. canon, punctuation, or style.

sequence.description: Stable ordering copied from stream events where available.

label.description: Short chip label such as `Думал 12 сек`, `Поиск`, or `3 фрагмента`.

content.description: Assistant/user text. Assistant text may contain trigger markers such as `::ck-trigger{kind="reader_references" artifactId="..."}` or `::ck-trigger{kind="agent_suggestions_ready" chapterId="..."}`. The client turns those markers into interactive UI by fetching the relevant resource.

parts.description: Persisted render parts for reasoning blocks, tool chips, trigger markers, and visible text after the stream completes.

contextLocators.description: Explicit reader/draft context selected by the user or UI before sending the agent request.

stream.description: When true, return a normal LLM `text/event-stream`. When false, return a completed JSON turn.

LlmStreamEvent.description: Provider-neutral JSON data payload for ordinary LLM text/event-stream chunks. Domain objects are not special stream events; they are referenced by trigger markers in text.

sequence.description: Monotonic per-stream event sequence used by clients to render stable reasoning/tool/text order.

delta.description: Assistant-visible text token delta. Trigger markers, when present, appear in this ordinary text.
Reasoning/status text delta shown in the agent/reasoning area when the selected provider exposes it.
Optional short display payload for the tool chip.
Human-readable tool result summary for the stream UI.

assistantMessageId.description: Persisted assistant message id created for this completed turn.

message.description: Human-readable error message safe to show in the chat UI.

marker.description: Exact marker present in `ChatMessage.content`, for example `::ck-trigger{kind="reader_references" artifactId="..."}`.
Exact marker present in `ChatMessage.content`, for example `::ck-trigger{kind="agent_suggestions_ready" chapterId="..." suggestionBatchId="..."}`.

suggestionBatchId.description: Present for `agent_suggestions_ready` when the backend created a batch of suggestions for one assistant turn.

suggestionIds.description: Specific backend-created suggestions referenced by this trigger.

readerTarget.description: Optional initial reader pane location selected by the assistant.

AgentDiffLocator.description: Draft locator precise enough to render and apply an agent diff hunk.

baseChapterRevision.description: Chapter revision used by the backend/agent when it created this suggestion.

batchId.description: Suggestion batch created by one assistant turn or backend agent run.

sourceMessageId.description: Assistant message that triggered the backend-created suggestion, when available.

contextQuote.description: Selected or surrounding draft text used as visible context for the suggestion card.

expectedChapterRevision.description: Current chapter revision known by the client before applying the suggestion.

prompt.description: Editor instruction describing the requested draft change.

expectedChapterRevision.description: Chapter revision the editor is working against; backend returns 409 if stale.

selectionQuote.description: Optional selected draft text the suggestion should target.

chapter.description: Updated chapter after accept; null after reject.
